options.C: Approve payment of an invoice from a supplier
options.D: Request a credit note from a supplier for returned goods

questions.explanation: A purchase order (PO) is an official document issued by the buyer to a supplier authorising the purchase of specific goods at an agreed price — a legally binding contract once accepted.

questions.question: A goods received note (GRN) is prepared when

options.A: Goods are dispatched from the warehouse to a customer
options.B: Goods are received from a supplier — to record, check, and confirm that the correct goods in the right quantity have arrived
options.C: Goods are returned to a supplier for being defective
options.D: An invoice is received from a supplier and approved for payment

questions.explanation: A GRN is prepared when goods arrive from a supplier, recording what was received and checking it against the purchase order — notifying accounts for payment processing.

questions.question: An invoice authorisation procedure requires that invoices from suppliers are

options.A: Paid immediately on receipt without further checks
options.B: Checked against the purchase order and goods received note before being approved for payment
options.C: Filed alphabetically by supplier name without any verification
options.D: Sent directly to the bank for electronic payment

questions.explanation: The three-way matching procedure checks the supplier's invoice against the purchase order and goods received note before authorising payment — preventing overpayment and fraud.

questions.question: A tender in business is a document in which

options.A: A supplier sends an invoice to the buyer after goods have been delivered
options.B: A potential supplier formally offers to supply specified goods or services at a stated price in response to an invitation
options.C: A buyer acknowledges receipt of goods from a supplier
options.D: A company formally requests a bank loan

questions.explanation: A tender is a formal, competitive offer submitted by a supplier in response to an Invitation to Tender — specifying price and terms under which they are willing to supply goods or services.

questions.question: An authorisation procedure in an office is used to

options.A: Automatically approve all routine purchases without manual review
options.B: Ensure that transactions and actions require approval from the appropriate level of authority before they proceed
options.C: Record all purchases made by each department during the year
options.D: Allow only senior management to make any decisions

questions.explanation: An authorisation procedure specifies who has the authority to approve different types of transactions at different value thresholds — ensuring control and accountability.

questions.question: A centralised office services department provides services such as typing, filing, and photocopying to

options.A: Only senior management in the organisation
options.B: All departments throughout the organisation from one central unit
options.C: External clients of the organisation
options.D: Only the finance and accounts department

questions.explanation: A centralised services department provides administrative services to all departments from one location — achieving specialisation, economies of scale, and consistency.

questions.question: The main advantage of centralised office services is

options.A: Each department has complete control over its own administrative support
options.B: Economies of scale, specialist staff, better equipment utilisation, and consistency of work quality
options.C: Faster access to services for individual departments
options.D: Greater flexibility and personalisation of services for each department

questions.explanation: Centralised services allow investment in better equipment, employ specialist staff, achieve economies of scale, ensure consistent quality, and reduce duplication.

questions.question: A reprographics department in an office is responsible for

options.A: Repairing and maintaining office equipment
options.B: Producing multiple copies of documents — including photocopying, printing, and duplicating
options.C: Managing the company's graphic design and branding
options.D: Recruiting and inducting new office staff

questions.explanation: The reprographics (copying) department handles bulk reproduction of documents — photocopying, digital printing — serving all departments needing multiple copies.

questions.question: A salary is a form of remuneration that is

options.A: Paid daily based on hours worked
options.B: A fixed regular payment (usually monthly) that does not vary with hours worked
options.C: A payment based on the number of units produced
options.D: A commission earned on each successful sale made

questions.explanation: A salary is a fixed regular payment (usually monthly) agreed in the employment contract, not varying with actual hours worked. Common for white-collar (office) workers.

questions.question: A wage is a form of remuneration that is usually

options.A: A fixed monthly payment regardless of hours worked
options.B: Calculated on an hourly or daily basis — varying with hours actually worked
options.C: A percentage of the company's annual profit
options.D: A one-off payment made at the end of the year

questions.explanation: A wage is hourly or daily pay for manual or part-time workers, calculated on actual hours worked (plus overtime). Unlike salaries, wages vary with hours worked.

questions.question: Commission as a form of remuneration is

options.A: A fixed monthly payment for all employees
options.B: A payment based on a percentage of the sales achieved by an employee
options.C: An allowance paid to cover the cost of business travel
options.D: A bonus paid to all employees at the end of the financial year

questions.explanation: Commission is performance-related pay calculated as a percentage of sales made or targets achieved, incentivising employees — especially salespeople — to increase productivity.

questions.question: A bonus payment is best described as

options.A: The basic salary paid to all employees each month
options.B: An additional payment over and above the regular salary, awarded for good performance or company profitability
options.C: A government-mandated payment for working overtime
options.D: Money deducted from an employee's salary for late arrival

questions.explanation: A bonus is an extra payment given to employees for good individual or company performance — motivating staff and rewarding contribution above the basic level.

questions.question: A pay slip is a document given to an employee to show

options.A: The amount of commission they earned in the previous quarter
options.B: A detailed breakdown of gross pay, deductions (tax, pension, NHF), and net pay for the pay period
options.C: The organisation's total wage bill for the month
options.D: The employee's performance rating and annual review results

questions.explanation: A pay slip shows earnings (basic pay, overtime, allowances), deductions (income tax, pension, NHF), and the resulting net (take-home) pay for each pay period.

questions.question: Time management in an office refers to

options.A: Recording the exact time each employee arrives and leaves work
options.B: Organising and planning how to divide available time between specific activities to maximise productivity
options.C: Setting strict deadlines for all office tasks regardless of complexity
options.D: Reducing the number of hours worked to cut staffing costs

questions.explanation: Time management involves planning, prioritising, and organising tasks — using to-do lists, diaries, and scheduling — to make the best use of available time and meet deadlines.

questions.question: A diary (appointments book) in office management is used to

options.A: Record the financial transactions of the organisation
options.B: Schedule, record, and remind an executive of appointments, meetings, deadlines, and tasks
options.C: Store the names and contact details of all company clients
options.D: Record the daily attendance of all staff

questions.explanation: A diary (physical or electronic) schedules appointments, meetings, and tasks, serving as a reminder system to ensure commitments are not missed.

questions.question: Work measurement in an office is used to

options.A: Measure the physical dimensions of the office space
options.B: Establish the time required to carry out a specific task to an acceptable standard — used for staffing and planning
options.C: Measure the productivity of the entire organisation
options.D: Record the hours worked by each employee each week

questions.explanation: Work measurement establishes standard times for tasks — enabling management to plan staffing levels, set performance targets, compare efficiency, and identify improvement areas.

questions.question: A press release is a written communication sent to

options.A: All employees of the organisation
options.B: The media (newspapers, TV, radio, online) to announce news, events, or developments about the organisation
options.C: Government regulatory authorities to report compliance
options.D: Suppliers and creditors about changes in payment terms

questions.explanation: A press release is written for the media — announcing company news with the aim of generating editorial coverage and publicity.

questions.question: A newsletter in an organisation is used to

options.A: Communicate urgent instructions from management to staff
options.B: Regularly inform employees, customers, or members about news, events, and developments within the organisation
options.C: Record the minutes of departmental meetings
options.D: Process orders from customers

questions.explanation: A newsletter is a regular publication that keeps employees, customers, or members informed about organisational news, events, achievements, and updates.

questions.question: A notice board in an office is used to

options.A: Display the organisation's financial results for the year
options.B: Post general information, announcements, and notices for the attention of all staff
options.C: Show the performance ratings of individual employees
options.D: Record attendance and absence of staff

questions.explanation: A notice board is a display board where general information — meeting notices, policy changes, job vacancies, event announcements — can be posted for all staff to see.

questions.question: An instruction manual in an office is a document used to

options.A: Record the instructions given by management at team meetings
options.B: Provide step-by-step guidance on how to operate equipment or carry out a procedure
options.C: List the disciplinary instructions for all employees
options.D: Record the training activities completed by each employee

questions.explanation: An instruction manual provides detailed, step-by-step guidance on how to operate equipment or carry out specific procedures — ensuring correct and safe use.

questions.question: Formal communication in an organisation is characterised by

options.A: Rumours and gossip passed between employees informally
options.B: Following established channels and procedures — documented, authorised, and part of the official communication system
options.C: Communication that takes place only in writing
options.D: Communication reserved for senior management only

questions.explanation: Formal communication follows official organisational structure and channels — memos, official letters, reports, meetings. It is authorised, documented, and governed by organisational policies.

questions.question: Effective listening in the office is important because

options.A: It allows the listener to plan what to say next while the speaker talks
options.B: It ensures the listener understands the message accurately, reducing errors, misunderstandings, and the need for clarification
options.C: It shows respect only to senior managers
options.D: It is a legal requirement under employment law

questions.explanation: Active listening — giving full attention, not interrupting, and confirming understanding — ensures messages are received accurately, reduces errors, and builds better working relationships.

questions.question: An email signature in business communication serves to

options.A: Sign an email digitally to confirm its authenticity
options.B: Provide the sender's name, position, company, and contact details automatically at the end of every email
options.C: Encrypt the email to prevent unauthorised access
options.D: Confirm that the email has been read by the recipient

questions.explanation: An email signature automatically appends the sender's name, job title, company, and contact details to outgoing emails — giving a professional impression.

questions.question: An executive's diary management includes

options.A: Recording all financial transactions made by the executive
options.B: Scheduling appointments, meetings, deadlines, and travel arrangements — and reminding the executive of upcoming commitments
options.C: Ordering stationery and office supplies for the executive
options.D: Filing all correspondence received by the executive

questions.explanation: Diary management involves maintaining the executive's schedule — booking and confirming appointments, ensuring no conflicts, and coordinating meetings and travel.

questions.question: An office cashier's duties include

options.A: Designing the company's financial strategy and investment portfolio
options.B: Receiving and disbursing cash, maintaining cash records, and ensuring cash is kept securely
options.C: Auditing the company's annual financial accounts
options.D: Managing the company's relationships with its banks

questions.explanation: An office cashier handles cash receipts and payments, maintains a cash book, reconciles cash holdings, issues receipts, and ensures cash is kept safely.

questions.question: A cheque requisition form is used to

options.A: Request a refund from a supplier for returned goods
options.B: Formally request that the accounts department prepare a cheque for a specific payment
options.C: Order goods from a supplier on credit
options.D: Authorise the bank to pay a standing order

questions.explanation: A cheque requisition form is an internal document used by a department to formally request the accounts department to issue a cheque — providing details of payee, amount, and reason.

questions.question: Microfilming is a records management technique that involves

options.A: Filing documents in alphabetical order using a microscope
options.B: Photographing documents at a greatly reduced size onto photographic film for compact storage
options.C: Shredding and micronising documents for disposal
options.D: Scanning documents and storing them on a microchip

questions.explanation: Microfilming photographs documents at greatly reduced size onto microfilm or microfiche. Large volumes can be stored in very small space and read using a microfilm reader.

questions.question: A visible card index system stores

options.A: Documents on microfilm for compact storage
options.B: Cards in overlapping rows so the bottom edge of each card (containing key information) is always visible
options.C: Loose documents in alphabetical suspension files
options.D: Electronic records in a searchable database

questions.explanation: A visible card index (Kardex) holds cards in overlapping rows so the bottom edge of each card (with key details) is always visible — allowing quick scanning and retrieval.

questions.question: A cheque writer (cheque protector) is used to

options.A: Sign cheques automatically on behalf of the managing director
options.B: Imprint the amount on a cheque in ink and perforations that are difficult to alter fraudulently
options.C: Print bank statements for reconciliation
options.D: Verify that a cheque is valid before it is accepted

questions.explanation: A cheque writer/protector prints and perforates the amount on a cheque in a way that is very difficult to alter, protecting against fraudulent modification.

questions.question: A dictating machine (Dictaphone) is used to

options.A: Connect telephone calls between offices
options.B: Record spoken dictation that can later be typed by a secretary or transcribed by voice recognition software
options.C: Print labels and address stickers
options.D: Sort incoming mail into categories automatically

questions.explanation: A dictating machine allows an executive to record dictation verbally, which a secretary later transcribes — saving the executive time and allowing dictation when the secretary is unavailable.

questions.question: An intercom system in an office is used to

options.A: Connect the office to external telephone networks
options.B: Facilitate internal voice communication between different rooms, floors, or departments within a building
options.C: Record all telephone conversations for security purposes
options.D: Broadcast company announcements over public radio

questions.explanation: An intercom (intercommunication) system allows immediate voice communication between different locations within the same building without using external phone lines.

questions.question: A paper folding machine in an office is used to

options.A: Staple pages of a document together
options.B: Automatically fold letters, circulars, or leaflets for insertion into envelopes — saving time and ensuring consistency
options.C: Cut paper to specific sizes using a preset template
options.D: Punch holes in paper for filing in ring binders

questions.explanation: A paper folding machine folds letters and circulars to the correct format automatically and consistently — saving significant time in large mail-out operations.

questions.question: A facsimile (fax) transmission is used to send

options.A: Electronic packages via internet courier services
options.B: An exact electronic image of a document over telephone lines to a receiving fax machine
options.C: Bulk emails to many recipients simultaneously
options.D: Voice messages to telephone answering machines

questions.explanation: A fax transmits a scanned image of a document over telephone lines — used when a handwritten signature or exact document image must be transmitted immediately.

questions.question: Feedback in communication is important because

options.A: It allows the sender to speak for longer
options.B: It confirms whether the message has been received and understood correctly, enabling the sender to correct misunderstandings
options.C: It is a legal requirement for all business communications
options.D: It reduces the cost of communication for the organisation

questions.explanation: Feedback closes the communication loop — it tells the sender how the message was received, enabling corrections to misunderstandings and ensuring the communication achieved its purpose.

questions.question: Accountability in management means

options.A: The responsibility of an employee to carry out assigned tasks
options.B: Being answerable to a superior for the exercise of authority and the performance of duties
options.C: The ability to count and manage financial resources
options.D: The process of reviewing employee performance annually

questions.explanation: Accountability means being answerable for the use of authority and the fulfilment of responsibilities. A manager who delegates tasks remains accountable to their superiors for outcomes.

questions.question: Office layout refers to

options.A: The colour scheme and decoration of the office
options.B: The design and arrangement of office furniture, equipment, and staff to maximise efficiency
options.C: The number of staff employed in the office
options.D: The hours during which the office is open for business

questions.explanation: Office layout is the physical arrangement of furniture, equipment, partitions, and people within the office space, designed to facilitate workflow, communication, and efficiency.

questions.question: The subject line in a business letter or memo is used to

options.A: Greet the recipient formally
options.B: State the topic or purpose of the communication briefly
options.C: Give the full name of the sender
options.D: Show the postal address of the recipient

questions.explanation: The subject line briefly states the topic of the communication, allowing the reader to immediately understand the purpose before reading the full content.

questions.question: Dismissal (termination) of employment means

options.A: An employee voluntarily resigning from their position
options.B: The employer ending the employee's employment — either for misconduct, poor performance, or other valid reasons
options.C: An employee taking unpaid leave for an extended period
options.D: A mutual agreement to end the employment relationship

questions.explanation: Dismissal is when the employer ends the employee's contract. It must follow proper procedures and be for a valid reason to avoid claims of unfair dismissal.

questions.question: Work scheduling in an office means

options.A: Deciding when staff can take annual leave
options.B: Planning and organising tasks, activities, and staff time to ensure work is completed efficiently and on time
options.C: Scheduling meetings and conferences for the management team
options.D: Recording the actual hours worked by each employee each day

questions.explanation: Work scheduling involves planning what work needs to be done, assigning it to staff, and setting deadlines — ensuring efficient use of time and resources.

questions.question: A memo is different from a business letter in that a memo

options.A: Is longer and more formal than a business letter
options.B: Is used for internal communication within the same organisation and does not require a salutation or formal closing
options.C: Must always be signed by the managing director before circulation
options.D: Can only be sent by post, not electronically

questions.explanation: A memo is used internally within the organisation and has a simplified format — no salutation, no complimentary close, just TO, FROM, DATE, SUBJECT, and the message body.

questions.question: A first aid kit in an office should be

options.A: Kept in the managing director's personal office for security
options.B: Readily accessible in the workplace, regularly checked and replenished, and staff trained in basic first aid
options.C: Used only by the company nurse or medical officer
options.D: Replaced only when items expire

questions.explanation: First aid kits must be accessible to all staff, regularly inspected and restocked, clearly signposted, and key staff should be trained in first aid.

questions.question: Electronic mail (email) in the office is used to

options.A: Physically deliver packages to clients
options.B: Send and receive written messages, documents, and files electronically over the internet
options.C: Print documents directly from a computer
options.D: Store digital copies of all office stationery

questions.explanation: Email allows instant sending and receiving of text messages and file attachments electronically — replacing much of the traditional postal correspondence.

questions.question: A requisition form for office supplies is used to

options.A: Order goods from an external supplier
options.B: Formally request the issue of supplies from the stationery store within the organisation
options.C: Record all stationery received from suppliers
options.D: Approve payment for stationery purchases

questions.explanation: A requisition form is an internal document used by a department to formally request items from the stationery or stores department.

questions.question: An office procedures manual is important because it

options.A: Allows the managing director to personally supervise every task
options.B: Provides a reference guide ensuring tasks are performed consistently, correctly, and efficiently — especially by new staff
options.C: Eliminates the need for any staff training
options.D: Gives employees freedom to perform tasks in any way they choose

questions.explanation: A procedures manual documents how tasks should be performed, ensuring consistency, quality, and compliance — especially valuable for training new staff and maintaining standards.

questions.question: The Workplace Regulations require that the temperature in an office should be

options.A: Below 15 degrees Celsius to maintain staff alertness
options.B: At least 16 degrees Celsius (or 13 degrees Celsius for physical work) — ensuring staff are comfortable and can work effectively
options.C: Exactly 25 degrees Celsius at all times
options.D: Set by individual employees using personal fans and heaters

questions.explanation: Standard workplace regulations require a minimum indoor working temperature of 16 degrees Celsius (or 13 degrees for physically active work) to maintain staff comfort and productivity.

questions.question: A mail register (postage book) is used to

options.A: Store all incoming letters in alphabetical order
options.B: Record details of outgoing mail — including the recipient, postage charged, and date dispatched
options.C: List all staff authorised to send official letters
options.D: Show the addresses of all company clients

questions.explanation: A mail register records all outgoing correspondence — date, addressee, type of mail, and postage cost — helping control postage expenditure and providing evidence if queries arise.

questions.question: The internet in an office is used for

options.A: Only sending and receiving emails
options.B: Accessing information, communicating, conducting research, online transactions, and cloud-based services
options.C: Only connecting office computers within the same building
options.D: Automatically backing up all company data

questions.explanation: The internet enables access to vast information, communication (email, video calls), online transactions, research, and cloud computing — transforming how modern offices operate.

questions.question: An executive's travel arrangements managed by a secretary include

options.A: Only booking the flight and hotel
options.B: Booking transport and accommodation, preparing itineraries, obtaining visas, arranging currency, and preparing travel documents
options.C: Only arranging meetings at the destination
options.D: Filing all travel receipts after the trip

questions.explanation: A secretary arranging business travel handles: transport bookings (flights, trains, taxis), hotel accommodation, detailed itineraries, visas/passports, foreign currency, and preparing all necessary documents.

questions.question: Line and staff organisation differs from a pure line organisation in that

options.A: In line and staff, all managers have equal authority
options.B: Line and staff adds specialist advisory (staff) positions to support line managers without taking away their authority
options.C: In line and staff, staff managers have authority over line managers
options.D: Line and staff eliminates the need for a chain of command

questions.explanation: Line and staff organisation retains the clear authority of line managers but adds staff specialists (HR, legal, finance advisers) who provide expert advice without having direct authority over line employees.

questions.question: A chairman's report at an AGM is used to

options.A: Record the minutes of the previous AGM
options.B: Give shareholders a review of the company's performance and prospects from the board's perspective
options.C: Show the detailed financial accounts of the company
options.D: List the agenda items for the AGM

questions.explanation: The chairman's report reviews the company's performance during the year, highlights key achievements and challenges, and looks ahead to future prospects — from the board's perspective.

questions.question: Centralised purchasing in an organisation means

options.A: Each department purchases its own supplies independently
options.B: All purchasing for the entire organisation is handled by one central purchasing department
options.C: Only the finance department can approve purchases
options.D: Purchasing is contracted out to an external procurement agency

questions.explanation: Centralised purchasing concentrates all buying decisions in one department — achieving bulk purchase discounts, consistent supplier relationships, standardised specifications, and better control over expenditure.

questions.question: Matrix management structure is one where

options.A: All staff report to only one manager in a strict hierarchy
options.B: Employees report to both a functional manager and a project manager simultaneously
options.C: Decision-making authority is distributed equally among all staff
options.D: The organisation is structured by geographic territory

questions.explanation: A matrix structure overlays project teams on top of functional departments. Employees have a dual reporting relationship — to their functional manager (specialist) and their project manager (deliverable). It improves flexibility but can cause role conflict.

questions.question: Which of the following is the MOST effective way to communicate a confidential, detailed policy change to all staff?

options.A: Posting a notice on the office notice board
options.B: Issuing a formal written circular letter or memo to each staff member individually
options.C: Announcing it verbally at a casual gathering
options.D: Posting it on the company's public website

questions.explanation: A formal written circular or memo ensures that every staff member receives the same accurate, documented information. It provides a record of communication, ensures confidentiality, and allows staff to refer back to the details.
